repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem:
vim.hl.on_yank() only works for TextYankPost, not TextPutPost (#39741).

Solution:
- Introduce hl_op().
- Deprecate on_yank().

A similar feature was added to Vim in <https://github.com/vim/vim/commit/f45ed6465dd6d219b72c61758158dd4038a5ef95>.


### Example


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f4fc1f81-3c69-4ca3-acb2-6fb6ae7a5a70

comment_data.body:
the `on_xx` name doesn't really fit, that's why I suggested `hl_op` (highlight operation).

`on_event` will be confusing if/when we add `vim.on_event` for defining autocmds.

```suggestion
vim.hl.hl_op({opts})                                    *vim.hl.hl_op()*
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -2954,21 +2954,23 @@ vim.glob.to_lpeg({pattern})                               *vim.glob.to_lpeg()*
 ==============================================================================
 Lua module: vim.hl                                                    *vim.hl*
 
-vim.hl.on_yank({opts})                                      *vim.hl.on_yank()*
-    Highlight the yanked text during a |TextYankPost| event.
+vim.hl.on_event({opts})                                    *vim.hl.on_event()*

@@ -321,9 +321,32 @@ describe('vim.hl.on_yank', function()
       {1:~                                                           }|
                                                                   |
     ]])
-    -- Use a new vim.hl.on_yank() call to cancel the previous timer
+    -- Use a new vim.hl.on_event() call to cancel the previous timer
     exec_lua(function()
-      vim.hl.on_yank({ timeout = 0, on_macro = true, event = { operator = 'y' } })
+      vim.hl.on_event({ timeout = 0, on_macro = true, event = { operator = 'y' } })
+    end)
+  end)
+
+  it('highlights for TextPutPost', function()

comment_regular_data.body:
it should be doable to share some code between these two

Right now there are like 50 copy-pasted lines and just like 5 of them actually are changed (where part of them are just new variable names)

Besides readability, sharing the impl effectively means we actually don't need new tests, or only minor additional assertions. 

I would suggest maybe renaming `on_yank` to `hl_op` (for back-compat, `on_yank` will then just be a thin wrapper around `hl_op`). The actual work being done is that text is highlighted after an operation. So the operation should just be a parameter, `op: 'TextPutPost'|'TextYankPost'` ...

~`vim.hl.on`/`on_event`? (could later -- possibly, if reasonably requested -- add `TextChanged`)~

That's not quite it, since the event is handled by the (user-defined) autocommand. What `on_yank` handles -- besides boilerplate -- is
1. extracting the type of range from the event
2. using the correct marks for the event.

Is it likely to use this function outside an autocommand? Otherwise we could make the new `on_event` a one-time function that also sets up the autocommand (even less boilerplate, yay!).

> Is it likely to use this function outside an autocommand? Otherwise we could make the new `on_event` a one-time function that also sets up the autocommand (even less boilerplate, yay!).

Not sure I follow. But can we see what `hl_op` looks like and then explore another approach in a followup?

The PR is updated with a different approach.

The current implementation of `vim.hl.on_yank` requires `event.operator` to be `"y"` in order to highlight the yanked text:

https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/45e63ee3a85a18ab04e8ee8c23c7239ec141072a/runtime/lua/vim/hl.lua#L176-L177

For `TextPutPost`, `event.operator` is either `"p"` or `"P"`. The updated patch uses this to support both `TextYankPost` and `TextPutPost` in a single `vim.hl.on_event()` function.

The changes from the previous version of patch:

* The function `vim.hl.on_yank` is now `vim.hl.on_event`.
    * It uses `event.operator` to determine which event was triggered. This is needed to have a specific clear timer for each event, so if the user does a _put_ immediately after _yank_ (like `yyp`), both highlights are visible.
    * The data for the clear timer is stored in a table (keys: `yank`, `put`), instead of individual variables.

* `vim.hl.on_yank` is still present (just a wrapper for `on_event`) and marked as deprecated.

* There is only one added test, specific to `TextPutPost`.

* The highlights can now be enabled with:

    ```vim
    autocmd TextYankPost,TextPutPost * lua vim.hl.on_event {}
    ```

    Or, with different options:

    ```vim
    autocmd TextYankPost * lua vim.hl.on_event {higroup='IncSearch', timeout=200}
    autocmd TextPutPost * lua vim.hl.on_event {higroup='Visual', timeout=100}
    ```
The patch is updated. I applied both suggestions (the function is now `vim.hl.hl_op`), and rebased it against the current `master`.

The error in the CI seems to be unrelated to this patch:

```
FAILED   1 test, listed below:
FAILED   ...st_xdg_terminal/test/functional/terminal/window_spec.lua @ 175: :terminal window with 'statuscolumn' wraps text
...st_xdg_terminal/test/functional/terminal/window_spec.lua:175: Row 1 did not match.
Expected:
  |*{121:++ 7  }                                            |
  |*{121:++ 8  }abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQR|
  |*{121:++ 9  }STUVWXYZ                                    |
  |*{121:++10  }abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQR|
  |*{121:++11  }STUVWXYZrows: 6, cols: 44                   |
  |*{121:++12  }^                                            |
  |{5:-- TERMINAL --}                                    |
Actual:
  |*{121:++ 8  }                                            |
  |*{121:++ 9  }abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQR|
  |*{121:++10  }STUVWXYZ                                    |
  |*{121:++11  }abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQR|
  |*{121:++12  }STUVWXYZrows: 6, cols: 44                   |
  |*{121:++13  }^                                            |
  |{5:-- TERMINAL --}                                    |

[...]
stack traceback:
	test/functional/ui/screen.lua:917: in function '_wait'
	test/functional/ui/screen.lua:540: in function 'expect'
	...st_xdg_terminal/test/functional/terminal/window_spec.lua:175: in function <...st_xdg_terminal/test/functional/terminal/window_spec.lua:153>
```

Thanks. If we don't  feel good about `hl_op()` we can change it in a followup. Maybe `hl_event()`, idk. But I think avoiding the `on_` prefix is a good idea.

In the future please update the PR description + commit message, I did so this time.

title: feat(vim.hl): `vim.hl.hl_op()`
